title: Surgical table and method for use thereof
abstract: A surgical table includes a sagittal adjustment device for manipulating the position of a patient. The surgical table includes a base portion including an upper surface for spacing the sagittal adjustment device from the ground. The sagittal adjustment device includes a first support portion and a second support portion. The first and second support portions are supported by and moveable over the upper surface of the base portion. The first and second support portions each include an upper surface configured to support portions of the body of the patient thereon. One of the first and second support portions is pivotally attached to the base portion, and moveable between a first position and a second position. The pivotal movement between the first and second positions of the one of the first and second portions serves in repositioning the body of patient to manipulate the spine of the patient.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A surgical table including a sagittal adjustment device for manipulating the position of a patient comprising: a base portion including an upper surface, and the base portion spacing the sagittal adjustment device from the ground; and the sagittal adjustment device including a first support portion and a second support portion, the first and second support portions being supported by and moveable over the upper surface of the base portion, the first and second support portions each including an upper surface, the upper surfaces of the first and second support portions being configured to support portions of the body of the patient thereon, one of the first and second support portions being pivotally attached to the base portion, the one of the first and second support portions being pivotally moveable between a first position and a second position, wherein pivotal movement between the first and second positions of the one of the first and second portions serves in repositioning the body of patient to manipulate the spine of the patient. 2. The surgical table of claim 1 , wherein the first and second support portions each include a lower surface, the lower surfaces of the first and second support portions affording movement of the first and second support surfaces on the upper surface of the base portion. 3. The surgical table of claim 1 , further comprising omni-directional casters provided on the lower surfaces of the first and second support portions. 4. The surgical table of claim 1 , wherein the other of the first and second support portions is pivotally attached to the base portion. 5. The surgical table of claim 1 , wherein the other of the first and second support portions is selectively pinned to the base portion by a pin received in an aperture formed in the base portion, the other of the first and second support portions being pivotally moveable relative to the upper surface of the base portion when unpinned from the base portion, and the other of the first and second support portions being capable of unconstrained movement relative to the upper surface of the base portion when unpinned from the base portion. The surgical table of claim 5, wherein the pin includes a diameter, and the size of the aperture for receiving the pin approximates the diameter of the pin to afford constrained pivotal movement of the other of the first and second support portions relative to the base portion. The surgical table of claim 5, wherein the pin includes a diameter, and the size of the aperture for receiving the pin is larger than the diameter of the pin to afford semi-constrained pivotal movement of the other of the first and second support portions relative to the base portion. The surgical table of claim 1 , further comprising a handle portion attached to at least one of the first and second support portions, the handle affording manipulation of the at least one of the first and second support portions relative to the base portion. A surgical table including a sagittal adjustment device for manipulating the position of a patient comprising: a base portion including an upper surface, and the base portion spacing the sagittal adjustment device from the ground; and the sagittal adjustment device including a first support portion and a second support portion, the first and second support portions being supported by and moveable over the upper surface of the base portion, the first and second support portions each including an upper surface, the upper surfaces of the first and second support portions being configured to support portions of the body of the patient thereon, one of the first and second support portions being pivotally attached to the base portion, the one of the first and second support portions being pivotally moveable between a first position and a second position, the other of the first and second support portions being moveable relative to the base portion, the other of the first and second support portions being moveable between at least a first position and a second position, wherein pivotal movement between the first and second positions of the one of the first and second portions and movement between the first and second positions of the other of the first and second portions serve in repositioning the body of patient to manipulate the spine of the patient. 10. The surgical table of claim 9, wherein the first and second support portions each include a lower surface, and further comprising omni-directional casters provided on the lower surfaces of the first and second support portions. 1 1. The surgical table of claim 10, wherein the other of the first and second support portions is selectively pinned to the base portion by a pin received in an aperture formed in the base portion, the other of the first and second support portions being pivotally moveable relative to the upper surface of the base portion when pinned to the base portion, and the other of the first and second support portions being capable of unconstrained movement relative to the upper surface of the base portion when unpinned from the base portion. 12. The surgical table of claim 10, wherein the pin includes a diameter, and the size of the aperture for receiving the pin approximates the diameter of the pin to afford constrained pivotal movement of the other of the first and second support portions relative to the base portion. 13. The surgical table of claim 10, wherein the pin includes a diameter, and the size of the aperture for receiving the pin is larger than the diameter of the pin to afford semi-constrained pivotal movement of the other of the first and second support portions relative to the base portion.
